words.def: not straight
words.example: a piece of bent wire

words.def: next to or at the side of somebody/something
words.example: He sat beside her all night.

words.def: used for making an extra comment that adds to what you have just said
words.example: I don't really want to go. Besides, it's too late now.

words.def: an arrangement to risk money, etc. on the result of a particular event; the money that you risk in this way
words.example: to win/lose a bet

words.def: on the other side; further on
words.example: Snowdon and the mountains beyond were covered in snow.

words.def: a strong feeling in favour of or against one group of people, or one side in an argument, often not based on fair judgement
words.example: accusations of political bias in news programmes (= that reports are unfair and show favour to one political party)

words.def: an offer by a person or a company to pay a particular amount of money for something
words.example: bid for something, A German firm launched a takeover bid for the company.

words.def: a document that shows how much you owe somebody for goods or services
words.example: the phone/electricity/gas bill

words.def: 1 000 000 000; one thousand million
words.example: Worldwide sales reached 2.5 billion.

words.def: connected with the science of biology
words.example: the biological sciences

words.def: having a strong, unpleasant taste; not sweet
words.example: Black coffee leaves a bitter taste in the mouth.

words.def: responsibility for doing something badly or wrongly; saying that somebody is responsible for something
words.example: Why do I always get the blame for everything that goes wrong?

words.def: a large cover, often made of wool, used especially on beds to keep people warm
words.example: It’s cold tonight—can I have another blanket?

words.def: not able to see
words.example: Doctors think he will go blind.

words.def: a hard hit with the hand, a weapon, etc.
words.example: She received a severe blow on the head.

words.def: brave and confident; not afraid to say what you feel or to take risks
words.example: It was a bold move on their part to open a business in France.

words.def: an occasion when a bomb is dropped or left somewhere; the act of doing this
words.example: recent bombings in major cities

words.def: something that forms a connection between people or groups, such as a feeling of friendship or shared ideas and experiences
words.example: the bond that links us

words.def: an arrangement that you make in advance to buy a ticket to travel somewhere, go to the theatre, etc.
words.example: a booking form/hall/clerk

words.def: something that helps or encourages somebody/something
words.example: a great/tremendous/welcome boost

words.def: the line that divides two countries or areas; the land near this line
words.example: I live in a small town in the US, near the Canadian border.

words.def: certain or likely to happen, or to do or be something
words.example: There are bound to be changes when the new system is introduced.

words.def: either of the two round soft parts at the front of a woman’s body that produce milk when she has had a baby
words.example: She put the baby to her breast.

words.def: baked clay used for building walls, houses and other buildings; an individual block of this
words.example: The school is built of brick.

words.def: lasting only a short time; short
words.example: a brief visit/meeting/conversation

words.def: for a short time
words.example: He had spoken to Emma only briefly.

words.def: wide
words.example: a broad street/avenue/river

words.def: a radio or television programme
words.example: (British English), a party political broadcast (= for example, before an election)

words.def: a person whose job is presenting or talking on television or radio programmes
words.example: She is a writer and broadcaster on environmental matters.

words.def: generally, without considering details
words.example: Broadly speaking, I agree with you.

words.def: the money that is available to a person or an organization and a plan of how it will be spent over a period of time
words.example: an annual budget of £10 million

words.def: any small insect
words.example: There's a bug crawling up your arm.

words.def: a small metal object that is fired from a gun
words.example: He was found to have a single bullet wound in his chest.

words.def: a number of things of the same type which are growing or fastened together
words.example: a bunch of bananas, grapes, etc.

words.def: an injury or a mark caused by fire, heat or acid
words.example: to have minor/severe/third-degree burns

words.def: a plant that grows thickly with several hard stems coming up from the root
words.example: a rose/holly bush

words.def: used to introduce a word or phrase that contrasts with what was said before
words.example: I got it wrong. It wasn't the red one but the blue one.

words.def: a small room on a ship in which you live or sleep
words.example: I lay in my cabin feeling miserably seasick.

words.def: a set of wires, covered in plastic or rubber, that carries electricity, phone signals, etc.
words.example: fibre-optic cable

words.def: to use numbers to find out a total number, amount, distance, etc.
words.example: calculate something, An independent valuer will calculate the value of your property.

words.def: a series of planned activities that are intended to achieve a particular social, commercial or political aim
words.example: an anti-smoking campaign

words.def: a long straight passage dug in the ground and filled with water for boats and ships to travel along; a smaller passage used for carrying water to fields, crops, etc.
words.example: the Panama/Suez Canal

words.def: to decide that something that has been arranged will not now take place
words.example: All flights have been cancelled because of bad weather.

words.def: a serious disease in which growths of cells, also called cancers, form in the body and kill normal body cells. The disease often causes death.
words.example: breast/lung cancer

words.def: a person who is trying to be elected or is applying for a job
words.example: a presidential candidate

words.def: a round stick of wax with a piece of string (called a wick) through the middle that is lit to give light as it burns
words.example: a flickering candle

words.def: having the ability or qualities necessary for doing something
words.example: capable of something, You are capable of better work than this.

words.def: the number of things or people that a container or space can hold
words.example: a fuel tank with a maximum capacity of 50 litres

words.def: the act of capturing somebody/something or of being captured
words.example: He evaded capture for three days.
